title: Net Present Value
event: Reyes Co. considers a $200,000 investment returning $60,000 of net cash flow annually for 5 years. The required rate is 10% (PV annuity factor, 10%, 5 = 3.79079). Compute the net present value.

computationPaths.narration: NPV discounts every future dollar to today, then nets the cost — positive means the project beats the 10% hurdle.

memorize.body: NPV = PV of net cash inflows − initial investment. NPV ≥ 0 → the project earns at least the required rate.
NPV of exactly zero isn't failure — it means the project earns precisely the hurdle rate.

distractors.feedback: Undiscounted: 300,000 − 200,000. NPV discounts the future flows first: 227,447 − 200,000 = 27,447.
That's the PV of the inflows. NET present value subtracts the 200,000 investment: 27,447.
